title: Subscription Renewal Forecasting
app: Contract repository export (ServiceNow SPM, Ariba), marketplace billing
spl:
(index=contracts sourcetype="license:usage") OR (index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur")
| eval renewal_epoch=strptime(renewal_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), amount=tonumber(annual_cost_usd)
| where renewal_epoch > relative_time(now(),"+30d@d") AND renewal_epoch < relative_time(now(),"+365d@d")
| eval days_until=round((renewal_epoch-now())/86400,0)
| stats sum(amount) as renewal_spend by vendor, renewal_date, cost_center
| sort renewal_date
description: Forecasting renewal cash-out dates and contract values avoids surprise budget hits and gives procurement time to negotiate or consolidate vendors.
value: Forecasting renewal cash-out dates and contract values avoids surprise budget hits and gives procurement time to negotiate or consolidate vendors.
implementation: Load subscription end dates and annual amounts from CLM or reseller exports; for AWS/Azure/GCP, tag marketplace subscriptions. Alert 90/60/30 days before renewal. Combine with utilization metrics to decide downgrade options before signing.
visualization: Timeline (renewals by quarter), Table (upcoming renewals), Single value (12-month renewal liability).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Contract repository export (ServiceNow SPM, Ariba), marketplace billing.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=license:usage`, `sourcetype=aws:billing:cur`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Load subscription end dates and annual amounts from CLM or reseller exports; for AWS/Azure/GCP, tag marketplace subscriptions. Alert 90/60/30 days before renewal. Combine with utilization metrics to decide downgrade options before signing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=contracts sourcetype="license:usage") OR (index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur")
| eval renewal_epoch=strptime(renewal_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), amount=tonumber(annual_cost_usd)
| where renewal_epoch > relative_time(now(),"+30d@d") AND renewal_epoch < relative_time(now(),"+365d@d")
| eval days_until=round((renewal_epoch-now())/86400,0)
| stats sum(amount) as renewal_spend by vendor, renewal_date, cost_center
| sort renewal_date
```

Understanding this SPL

**Subscription Renewal Forecasting** — Forecasting renewal cash-out dates and contract values avoids surprise budget hits and gives procurement time to negotiate or consolidate vendors.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=license:usage`, `sourcetype=aws:billing:cur`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Contract repository export (ServiceNow SPM, Ariba), marketplace billing. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: contracts, cloud_billing; **sourcetype**: license:usage, aws:billing:cur. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=contracts, index=cloud_billing, sourcetype="license:usage". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **renewal_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where renewal_epoch > relative_time(now(),"+30d@d") AND renewal_epoch < relative_time(now(),"+365d@d")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_until** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by vendor, renewal_date, cost_center** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
compare with Cost Explorer; compare with ServiceNow ITAM; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (renewals by quarter), Table (upcoming renewals), Single value (12-month renewal liability).
grandmaExplanation: Cost and capacity tools watch how much we spend on cloud and the data center, and where we have spare or strained capacity.
knownFalsePositives: Forecasts vary with seasonal load, new project ramps, or M&A integration spending.
